diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Duplication 17p10-p12 associated with a dicentric chromosome
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on cytogenetic and fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) analysis, which revealed a partial duplication of chromosome 17p10-p12, including the entire Smith–Magenis syndrome (SMS) critical region and a portion of the Charcot–Marie–Tooth disease type 1A (CMT1A) critical region. The patient's clinical features, such as developmental delay, absence of speech, talipes, congenital dislocation of the hips, and signs of peripheral neuropathy consistent with CMT1A, supported the diagnosis. The duplication was confirmed to be of maternal origin through microsatellite polymorphism studies.
